admin.description: Optional migration admin. `None` ships a frozen instance.

min_answer_timeout_secs.description: Mandatory floor for `AskQuestion.answer_timeout_secs` (FM-8). Reality.eth has no contract-level floor; we add one because SuDAO and Gnosis Guild were drained partly because integrators chose 12h / 1h windows.

oneOf.description: Pose a new question. `initial_bond` is the floor on the very first answer's bond (Reality.eth's `min_bond`). Bonds are pinned to a single denom captured here.
Top up the bounty paid to the eventual winner. Permitted until finalization.
Submit an answer with a bond. `current_bond_seen` is the front-run guard — reject if the on-chain bond at execution time exceeds it.
Submit a competing answer (≥ 2× current bond). Same shape as `SubmitAnswer`; kept as a distinct variant so external indexers can distinguish "first answer in this round" from "counter-answer."

Configured-arbitrator-only. Requires the question to be OpenAnswered (and thus to have at least one answer), applies the optional `current_bond_seen` front-run guard, freezes it, and starts the arbitration deadline.

Pending-arbitration-only. The configured arbitrator may cancel at any time; anyone may cancel at or after the arbitration deadline. Unfreezes the question and resets `finalize_ts` to `now + timeout` (re-extend, not restore).

Arbitrator-only and pending-arbitration-only. Finalizes with any `winning_answer` bytes chosen by the arbitrator and records the validated `payee` as that answer's zero-bond history entry. No submitted-history membership or answer-schema check is performed.

Drive the resumable claim walk. Anyone may call once finalized. `history_entries` is the caller-supplied history chain, newest-first; the contract verifies each entry against the stored chain tip (Reality.eth `claimWinnings` pattern). The caller may pass any prefix of the remaining chain; partial walks persist `cursor_hash` via the `CLAIMS` map and resume on the next call.
Drain the caller's pull-payment balance for the named denom.
Cw20 hook — wraps a state-mutating variant whose bond is a cw20.

AnswerSchemaFilter.description:
Per-question configuration of the answer-schema filter callout.

Stored on the `Question` at ask time. The contract address is captured at ask time so that later cw-filter migrations cannot brick existing questions (lessons §7.4). If `None`, no schema validation is performed and any payload of the declared `AnswerType` is accepted.

contract.description: cw-filter contract address used for this question. Captured at ask time so the question's validation surface is stable.

filter.description: JSON filter spec to run against every submitted answer.

AnswerType.description: Coarse answer type. The exact payload bytes are opaque to the contract; cw-filter validates schema per question.

Binary.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

Cw20ReceiveMsg.description: Cw20ReceiveMsg should be de/serialized under `Receive()` variant in a ExecuteMsg

HistoryEntry.description:
One step of the answer-history chain, supplied by the claimer.

`prev_hash` is the chain hash BEFORE this entry was added (i.e. the hash from which `prev_hash || answer || denom || bond || answerer || is_commitment` hashes back to the cursor). At step 0 (the first answer ever submitted) `prev_hash` is the all-zero hash.

oneOf.description: Contract instantiation parameters.
Full question state.
The finalized winning answer, or error if not finalized.

Reader-side trust knob (Reality.eth's `getFinalAnswerIfMatches`). Reverts unless every supplied constraint passes — so a downstream consumer can require the question used at least the bond / timeout / denom it considers safe.
Paginated list of questions, optionally filtered by current state.
A user's pull-payment balance for the given denom.

Binary.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

State.description: The explicit state machine derived from Reality.eth's modifier set.

oneOf.description: Question does not exist.
Created but no answer yet.
At least one answer submitted; not in arbitration; not past `finalize_ts`.
Arbitrator froze the question via `RequestArbitration`.
`finalize_ts` reached (or arbitration finalized); `Claim` not yet drained the history.
History fully drained — all payouts credited.

config.title: Config
config.description: Per-instance configuration. Set at instantiation, never mutated.

admin.description: Instantiator / migration admin. Optional — `None` is a published-frozen instance with no upgrade path.

min_answer_timeout_secs.description: Mandatory floor for `answer_timeout_secs` on every `AskQuestion`. Reality.eth has no contract-level floor; SuDAO + Gnosis Guild were drained partly because their integrators chose 12h / 1h timeouts (FM-8).

min_initial_bond_floor.description: Mandatory floor for `initial_bond` on every `AskQuestion`. Defends against tiny-bond griefing (FM-1).

Addr.description:
A human readable address.

In Cosmos, this is typically bech32 encoded. But for multi-chain smart contracts no assumptions should be made other than being UTF-8 encoded and of reasonable length.

This type represents a validated address. It can be created in the following ways 1. Use `Addr::unchecked(input)` 2. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_validate(input)?` 3. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_humanize(canonical_addr)?` 4. Deserialize from JSON. This must only be done from JSON that was validated before such as a contract's state. `Addr` must not be used in messages sent by the user because this would result in unvalidated instances.

This type is immutable. If you really need to mutate it (Really? Are you sure?), create a mutable copy using `let mut mutable = Addr::to_string()` and operate on that `String` instance.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

AnswerSchemaFilter.description:
Per-question configuration of the answer-schema filter callout.

Stored on the `Question` at ask time. The contract address is captured at ask time so that later cw-filter migrations cannot brick existing questions (lessons §7.4). If `None`, no schema validation is performed and any payload of the declared `AnswerType` is accepted.

contract.description: cw-filter contract address used for this question. Captured at ask time so the question's validation surface is stable.

filter.description: JSON filter spec to run against every submitted answer.

AnswerType.description: Coarse answer type. The exact payload bytes are opaque to the contract; cw-filter validates schema per question.

Binary.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

Question.description: A question's stored state. See lessons §1 for the precise transitions each handler asserts.

answer_schema.description: Per-question cw-filter binding. Captured at ask time so later cw-filter migrations cannot brick this question (lessons §7.4).

arbitration_deadline.description: Arbitration request deadline (set when `RequestArbitration` lands). If `None`, no arbitration request is outstanding.

arbitration_timeout_secs.description: Maximum time the arbitrator has between `RequestArbitration` and `SubmitArbitration` before a non-arbitrator may force-cancel (re-extension defense against a stalled arbitrator).

best_answer.description: Latest "best" answer (i.e. the answer of the highest-bonded round so far). `None` until the first answer lands.

bond_denom.description: Bond denom pinned at ask time. Every subsequent bond on this question must match (lessons §2.3).

bounty.description: Asker-supplied bounty (paid out to the latest right-answerer).

current_bond.description: Latest bond amount in the chain. Used for the 2× doubling guard.

finalize_ts.description: `block.time.seconds()` when the question will finalize, idle. `None` until the first answer lands.

is_claimed.description: Set when the question is fully claimed (`history_hash` exhausted on the resumable claim walk).

nonce.description: User-supplied nonce — enables an asker to re-pose the same question (lessons §8 q1).

opening_ts.description: Optional opening timestamp; question is not answerable until this time has elapsed (Reality.eth `opening_ts`).

round_count.description: Number of rounds (answers + arbitrator-submitted answers) so far.

State.description: The explicit state machine derived from Reality.eth's modifier set.

oneOf.description: Question does not exist.
Created but no answer yet.
At least one answer submitted; not in arbitration; not past `finalize_ts`.
Arbitrator froze the question via `RequestArbitration`.
`finalize_ts` reached (or arbitration finalized); `Claim` not yet drained the history.
History fully drained — all payouts credited.
